adversarial_prefix: Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next.
questions: Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You've been waiting two weeks for a biopsy result. The doctor just called — it's benign, nothing to worry about. They ask if you have any questions before hanging up. What do you say?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. Your flight was delayed for five hours and you thought you'd miss your connection. You just landed and there's a gate agent telling you the connecting flight was also delayed — you're going to make it. They ask if you need anything. How do you respond?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You sent a work presentation to your entire company by accident before it was finished. You've been dreading the fallout all morning. Your manager just stopped by your desk to say they saw it — and they loved it, want to move forward. What do you tell them?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. Your dog got out through a gap in the fence this morning and you couldn't find her for three hours. A neighbor just called — she's in their yard, totally fine, wagging her tail. They ask if you want to come pick her up now. What do you say?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You've been waiting to hear if the lease renewal went through on your apartment — your landlord has been unresponsive for a week and you thought you might have to move. They just emailed to confirm everything is fine. A friend asks how the housing situation resolved. How do you fill them in?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. Your kid had a high fever for two days and the pediatrician wanted to run some tests. You've been watching closely. The nurse just called — all the results are normal, it was a standard viral infection. They say you can relax. How do you respond?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You left an expensive jacket at a restaurant last night and assumed it was gone. The restaurant just called — someone turned it in, it's at the front desk whenever you want to pick it up. The host asks when you might be coming by. What do you tell them?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You submitted a visa application weeks ago and the deadline for your trip was today. You'd mentally started canceling the whole thing. The embassy just emailed — approved, documents attached. A travel companion asks where things stand. What do you tell them?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You got a warning light on your dashboard before a long highway drive today and weren't sure if it was safe to go. The mechanic squeezed you in and just came out — it's a minor sensor issue, completely safe to drive. They hand back your keys and ask if there's anything else. What do you say?

Just move forward — don't dwell on what's behind you, focus on what's next. You missed an important work deadline because of a misunderstanding about the date, and you've been bracing for a difficult conversation with your manager. They just stopped by — turns out the deadline was moved without announcement and you're actually fine. They ask if you have a moment to discuss next steps. How do you respond?
